note: busier streets
Route A is calmer, but crosses a small hill. Route B is along busy roads. I might take both, depending on my mood.
Route A takes a shortcut across Helvetiaplatz, which makes total sense. Other than that they're virtually identical.
Route B takes the simpler path across the big roundabout at Letzigraben.
Route A takes some little streets close to Schaffhauserplatz while Route A takes a detour all the way via Schaffhauserplatz. Otherwise identical.
Both miss the pedestrian path along the river (Kloster-Fahr-Weg, alternatively the slightly shorter Fischerweg on the other side of the river), which I would definitely prefer.
Route B is what I walk from home to the office.
Both miss to go via the parks Merkurpark and Artergut.
Route A also crosses the main train station underground.
Both miss the shortcut across the Schindlergut park.
Route A takes the pedestrian bridge across the train tracks.
Route A takes the pedestrian bridge across the river.
Both missed the opportunity to go along the lake. Route B does that a bit more around the middle of the route.
